metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1944
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors use paired pulse depression in GABAergic neurons of the CA1 region as an indicator of presynaptic site of action. They showed that application of a 5HT1a agonist in slices relieves depression and causes facilitation, as measured by eIPSC recordings. The authors also investigated the effects of the agonist in isolated nerve-bouton preparations to further validate the presynaptic site of action.

21/11/2017 Pim
- Term selected by expert ('integral component of presynaptic membrane (GO:0099056)') matches the interpretation of experimental data by the authors:

"We conclude that 5-HT inhibits GABAergic mIPSCs by inactivating the AC-cAMP signal transduction pathway via a G-protein-coupled 5-HT1A receptor and this intracellular pathway directly acts on the GABA-releasing process independent of K+ and Ca2+ channels in the presynaptic nerve terminals."

- Although BP data may provide misleading clues on the precise localization of the regulating protein I follow the opinion by the authors and expert in this matter on the presynaptic localization of the 5-HT1a protein in this system.. Experimental description: Wisar rats from 10-14 D old (Methods)
